tool_args.label: LGA AA Summary Public Meeting #2, November 2, 2011

payload.title: LaGuardia Airport Access Alternatives Analysis: Summary Public Meeting #2, November 2, 2011
payload.description: Summary of the second public meeting for the LaGuardia Airport Access Alternatives Analysis, held November 2, 2011 in workshop format with 23 participants

warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

payload.entity_name: New York City Department of Transportation

payload.description: Study examining Bus Rapid Transit (BRT) as the locally preferred mode for improving transit access to LaGuardia Airport
Bus route used to access LaGuardia Airport; participants expressed desire to see Q23 extended onto airport property
Bus route used to access LaGuardia Airport; participants expressed desire for Q48 to provide express service to the airport and to continue to LIJ Hospital and North Shore Towers
Bus route serving LaGuardia Airport; participants noted congestion on Junction Boulevard, bus bunching, and drivers not stopping to pick up passengers
Bus route serving LaGuardia Airport via 125th Street; participants noted bus bunching, crowding, and suggested a limited-stop airport-only route
Bus route serving LaGuardia Airport area; participants noted it is often backed up and no buses at 74th Street Terminal, often stuck behind sanitation vehicles
Bus route serving LaGuardia Airport area; participants noted it is very slow along Roosevelt Avenue
Bus route from Rockaway Park to LaGuardia Airport mentioned as a participant-identified route
Bus route mentioned in context of Q23 extension potentially helping relieve the Q44
Participant-identified route from northern Bronx to LGA via Robert F. Kennedy Bridge; also Bx55 mentioned
Participant-identified route from Midtown to LGA via Northern Boulevard
Participant-identified route from North Shore Towers to LGA (mentioned alongside Q48)

payload.event_name: LaGuardia Airport Access Alternatives Analysis Public Meeting #2
payload.description: Second public meeting for the LGA AA study, held in workshop format with 23 participants spread over four small groups

warnings: event.organizers is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Organizer lists refer to entity records. Suggested relation: organized_by.

payload.event_name: LaGuardia Airport Access Alternatives Analysis Public Meeting #1
payload.description: First public meeting for the LGA AA study, referenced as having presented material that was reinforced in Meeting #2

Features and amenities of the NYCDOT/NYCT BRT toolbox presented to workshop participants: Offset and Curbside Bus Lanes, Off-Board Fare Collection, Enhanced Stations, Real-time Arrival Information, BRT Vehicles, Subway-like Station Spacing, and Traffic Signal Priority for Buses
Off-board fare payment as a BRT feature; participants commented it would be particularly helpful and asked about its success in NYC

Offset and curbside bus lanes as BRT features; participants commented bus lanes do not work on congested local streets, asked where they would be, and suggested Junction Boulevard is good for bus lanes
Traffic Signal Priority for Buses as a BRT feature; participants asked how it works without a bus lane
Enhanced Stations as a BRT feature; participants preferred this in the survey
Real-time Bus Information as a BRT feature; participants preferred this in the survey

tool_args.label: BRT selected as locally preferred mode

payload.claim_text: The decision was made to move forward with Bus Rapid Transit (BRT) as the locally preferred mode since it fulfilled the three primary goals of the study: Improve Transit Mobility, Connectivity, and Accessibility to LaGuardia Airport; Improve Transit Service to Neighborhoods; Provide Cost Effective and Efficient Transport Options in a Short-term Timeframe
payload.subject: LaGuardia Airport Access Alternatives Analysis

tool_args.label: Only 7% of passengers arrive by bus

payload.claim_text: Only seven percent of passengers are arriving at LaGuardia by bus
payload.subject: LaGuardia Airport passenger access

tool_args.label: Majority of NYC passengers come from Manhattan

payload.claim_text: A majority of passengers (whose trip begins in New York City) are coming from Manhattan, followed by Brooklyn and Queens
payload.subject: LaGuardia Airport passenger origins

tool_args.label: Majority arrive by hired car or personal car

payload.claim_text: A majority of travelers arrive by hired car (such as taxi or black car) or van service, followed by personal cars
payload.subject: LaGuardia Airport passenger access modes

tool_args.label: Majority supported BRT or supported with concerns

payload.claim_text: Asked how they now felt about BRT as a solution to access LaGuardia Airport, a majority of the participants either supported it or supported with concerns
payload.subject: Workshop participants' view on BRT

tool_args.label: Most interest in route between airport and 74th St/Roosevelt Ave

payload.claim_text: When asked which neighborhood could benefit the most from a new BRT service, the route between the airport and the 74th Street/Roosevelt Avenue station through Jackson Heights drew the most interest
payload.subject: BRT route preference

tool_args.label: Preferred BRT features: Real Time Info, Enhanced Stations, Offset Bus Lanes, Off-Board Fare

payload.claim_text: The participants preferred Real Time Bus Information, Enhanced Stations, Off-set Bus Lanes and Off-Board Fair Collection. They did not prefer Curbside Bus Lanes.
payload.subject: BRT feature preferences

tool_args.label: Q72: 25 min AM, 45 min PM from Queens Blvd to LGA

payload.claim_text: Q72 Commuter: It is quick during the morning hours – 25 minutes from Queens Boulevard to the LGA. But Junction Boulevard is very congested in the afternoon. It can take 45 minutes to get from LGA back to Queens Boulevard during the afternoon hours.
payload.subject: Q72 travel time

warnings: claim.route is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Route ids should link to route records when supported. Suggested relation: route has_claim claim when the route endpoint resolves exactly.

tool_args.label: Q72 afternoon bus bunching: up to 45 min wait

payload.claim_text: Bus bunching in the afternoon often results in a wait as long as 45 minutes for the bus to pick passengers up at the terminal
payload.subject: Q72 bus bunching

warnings: claim.route is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Route ids should link to route records when supported. Suggested relation: route has_claim claim when the route endpoint resolves exactly.

payload.claim_text: The M60 often has bus bunching, with very long gaps between buses. Conditions on board are very crowded.
payload.subject: M60 service quality

warnings: claim.route is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Route ids should link to route records when supported. Suggested relation: route has_claim claim when the route endpoint resolves exactly.
